channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Private landowners fill the void as return of Aboriginal land stalls
item.description: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.
item.dc:creator: Adam Holmes

media:group.media:description: The property in Collinsvale will be used to train the next generation of rangers in land management.

item.title: Need for mine's hybrid power plant 'embarrassing', outback MP says
item.description: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.
item.dc:creator: Abbey Halter

media:group.media:description: At the end of its construction, the power facility at Eva Copper Mine will be about the size of 53 Melbourne Cricket Grounds.

item.title: Breaking: Austrian court finds man guilty in plot to attack Taylor Swift concert
item.description: The concert plot was thwarted, but Austrian authorities still cancelled Swift’s three performances in August 2024.

media:group.media:description: The man had planned to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna nearly two years ago.

item.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now
item.description: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Grattan

media:group.media:description: Outgoing NACC chief Paul Brereton believes in some ways he contributed to his own "downfall".

item.title: Details of first NZYQ Nauru deal payment revealed
item.description: Details of the secretive NZYQ resettlement deal have emerged, including a breakdown of the government's first payment.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Staszewska

media:group.media:description: The agreement covers 354 former detainees, collectively known as the NZYQ cohort, who were released into the Australian community.

item.title: What does it mean to be the best jack of all trades, but master of none?
item.description: Elite athletes are constantly striving for perfection — an extra millisecond shaved off their time or another centimetre on their distance — but for heptathletes like Tori West, the equation is a little bit harder.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Heptathlon is a gruelling two-day exhibition of athleticism.

item.title: 'I'm about to explode': UK hits 35C in spring as warning issued
item.description: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.
item.dc:creator: Mazoe Ford

media:group.media:description: London's spring heatwave was too much for some people.

item.title: Buses, brekkie and circus skills getting kids through the school gate
item.description: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.
item.dc:creator: Briana Fiore and Bill Ormonde

media:group.media:description: Two morning bus runs ensure as many students as possible can get to school.

item.title: How tax rules benefit top earners by $700,000 but median earners by $5,700
item.description: Commonwealth Treasury secretary Jenny Wilkinson says the average top income earner benefited from the existing investment tax arrangements to the tune of $700,000 over their lifetime, versus just $5,700 for median income earners.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda and Yiying Li

media:group.media:description: Jenny Wilkinson has defended the proposed tax changes, saying young people and average income earners would have been better off.

item.title: The 14 risk factors for dementia Jane wishes she knew earlier
item.description: Too many Australians still see dementia as an inevitability, according to new data but there is a list of steps you can take to reduce the risks.
item.dc:creator: Paige Cockburn

media:group.media:description: Jane Turner, 63, has been diagnosed with early onset Alzheimer's Disease.

item.title: Small business says Labor's CGT plan 'handcuffs' ambition
item.description: Small business owners are urging Labor to rethink more than just the eligibility thresholds for capital gains tax concessions.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Lane Stockton believes the CGT change "handcuffs" his ability to expand.

item.title: Insect repellent may not always keep mozzies away, study suggests
item.description: Scientists have found that mosquitoes in the lab can be trained to connect the smell of a widely used repellent with food, but repellents are still the best way to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Phiddian

media:group.media:description: Yellow fever mosquitoes carry a number of diseases including dengue fever, which kills roughly 40,000 people each year.

item.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons
item.description: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.
item.dc:creator: Koren Helbig

media:group.media:description: Koren Helbig prepares to plant her garlic.

item.title: The Aussie rocker who mastered the video game needle drop
item.description: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

media:group.media:description: Johnny Galvatron leans on his love of music with his games company's latest release, Mixtape.

item.title: Friday news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback
item.description: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

media:group.media:description: What makes words 'obscene'?

item.title: Trump says Murdoch indicated he would 'handle' newspaper's Epstein story
item.description: In refiling his lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal, the US president alleges Rupert Murdoch indicated a story about his ties with Jeffrey Epstein would not be published.

media:group.media:description: Rupert Murdoch and Donald Trump have had a bumpy relationship since the real estate mogul entered politics.

item.title: At least 16 students dead as fire tears through Kenyan girls school
item.description: The blaze reportedly started when a student set a mattress on fire inside the school's dormitory, with a further 79 students taken to hospital with injuries.

media:group.media:description: Authorities say the fire broke out around midnight Wednesday night or Thursday morning, local time.

item.title: Iran and US trade air strikes after Trump dismisses Hormuz deal
item.description: The US president has repeatedly said the end of the war is close, but told media at a cabinet meeting on Wednesday he was not yet satisfied by the negotiations and the US is not discussing easing sanctions, one of Tehran's key demands.

media:group.media:description: Kuwait, pictured in February, hosts a US air base, and has been the target of retaliatory strikes across the Gulf by Iran.

item.title: Adelaide man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks
item.description: A man has been charged for allegedly using trucks in serial petrol drive-offs north of Adelaide, with police estimating the cost to be more than $8,000.

media:group.media:description: A man allegedly used trucks to steal fuel in Adelaide's north over a period of seven months.

item.title: 'Those guys are gonna disappear': ICAC reveals 'Pink Ladies' texts
item.description: Messages obtained by the NSW corruption watchdog have revealed discussions by a group of long-time Sydney local government officials about plans to secure senior positions for themselves and friends at City of Parramatta Council.
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar

media:group.media:description: Angela Jones-Blayney told the corruption watchdog she was a long-time member of the “Pink Ladies”, a group who worked together at various Sydney councils.

item.title: AFP defends public arrest of Ben Roberts-Smith
item.description: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett detailed Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest following questions about its public nature.

item.title: People trapped in new housing estate traffic bottlenecks welcome funding
item.description: Motorists in Melbourne's outer north welcome funds for "lifeline" outer suburbs road, but say more needs to be done.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Paul

media:group.media:description: Mustafa Aljburi says driving is a daily headache for local motorists.

item.title: Ex-police officer admits sharing bed but denies rape, court hears
item.description: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Carbone

media:group.media:description: The former police officer is facing trial in South Australia's District Court.

item.title: Young police officer's death raises questions about mental health support
item.description: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.
item.dc:creator: Catherine James

media:group.media:description: An inquest into the self-inflicted death of a new police recruit has explored mental health support in the force.

item.title: Saints' tough loss made worse as midfielder injures Achilles
item.description: Hawthorn consolidates its top four spot with a big win over St Kilda, as the Saints lose Sam Flanders to a potentially serious Achilles injury.
item.dc:creator: Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: Sam Flanders' season looks over after the St Kilda midfielder suffered a suspected Achilles injury against Hawthorn at Docklands.
